items.headline: Iran War Updates: Key events in the last 24 hours
items.summary: According to information reported on April 3 and 4, 2026, the American-Israeli and Iranian war, now entering its fifth week, continues to escalate. A significant event during this period was the downing of two American warplanes by Iranian air defense systems…

items.headline: Tehran Hits Gulf Energy Sites, Trump Warns US 'Hasn't Even Started' To Destroy Iran
items.summary: Iran launched attacks across the Middle East on April 3, setting parts of a major Kuwaiti oil refinery ablaze and triggering air defense responses across the Gulf, as the war with the United States and Israel neared the end of its fifth week.

items.headline: Ceasefire talks reach standstill...
items.summary: Iran told mediators it is unwilling to meet US officials in Islamabad to discuss a ceasefire, as regional intermediaries such as Egypt and Turkey search for alternate solutions to end the conflict.

items.headline: Iranian state TV claims US fighter pilot ejected over southwestern Iran
items.summary: The report has not been independently verified, and US authorities, including Central Command and the White House, have not responded to the claim

items.headline: “Make A Deal Before It’s Too Late”: Trump Threatens Tehran (Again)
items.summary: World's most important oil price hits record high as Trump threatens Iran: "make a deal before it's too late."

items.headline: Japanese, French and Omani vessels cross Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Three Omani-operated tankers, a French-owned container ship and a Japanese-owned gas carrier have crossed the Strait ‌of Hormuz since Thursday, shipping data showed, reflecting Iran's policy to allow passage for vessels it deems friendly. Iran initially shut …

items.headline: 'Regime change' in the Strait of Hormuz sees 20,000 sailors stranded at sea
items.summary: Interviews and first-hand accounts from sailors stranded in the Persian Gulf — under threat of drone attacks, missiles, and exploding boats — reveal an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis unfolding.

items.headline: Iran: at least five ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz in the last 24 hours
items.summary:
Oil and commodity markets are anxiously looking for signs that traffic in the Straits is being restored

items.headline: US military vs Iran's 'arch defence': Dangerous new phase of war looms
items.summary: While Donald Trump talks about ending and winning the war in Iran, major US military assets are still on their way to the Middle East. These are the options they give him.

items.headline: What is Benjamin Netanyahu’s end game in the Iran war?
items.summary: The war in the Middle East is a huge political book for the Israeli prime minister in an election year – but it may come at a great cost.

items.headline: Putin Is Not Trapped: Why Regime Survival Does Not Depend on Victory
items.summary: For four years, analysts and policymakers have warned that Russian President Vladimir Putin “cannot afford to lose” in Ukraine. Increasingly, some argue he cannot afford peace either. In this view, the war is existential for his regime. Defeat would shatter P…

items.summary: Editor-in-Chief Atul Singh and FOI Senior Partner Glenn Carle, a retired CIA officer who now advises companies, governments and organizations on geopolitical risk, turn their attention eastward to examine the sharp escalation in China–Taiwan tensions over the…

items.headline: Will China join Pakistan-led efforts to mediate US-Iran peace?
items.summary: Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar visits China after Islamabad talks to broker negotiations between the US and Iran.

items.headline: The war with Iran may be ushering in a new nuclear age
items.summary: Global tensions are rising as nations consider developing nuclear weapons. Donald Trump's policies are prompting countries like Germany and Poland to explore their own nuclear deterrents. Meanwhile, China and Russia are concerned about proliferation in Japan …

items.headline: Russia Oil Revenues Halved in March Before War Boost
items.summary: Russian producers paid 494.9 billion rubles ($6.18 billion) in oil taxes last month, down 48 percent year on year, according to Bloomberg calculations based on Finance Ministry data released Friday.

items.headline: Russia’s Oil Revenues Halved in March Before Iran War Boost
items.summary: Russia’s oil tax revenue in March dropped by nearly half compared to a year earlier, highlighting the Kremlin’s financial strain just before the Middle East war delivered an unexpected boost to Moscow’s earnings.

items.headline: Oil prices surge while Asian share prices rise moderately
items.summary: Oil prices spike amid Iran war concerns as Asian markets show modest gains in cautious trading.

items.headline: World anxious to open Hormuz Strait while Trump and Iran trade threats
items.summary: Dozens of countries sought ways to restart vital energy shipments through the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump vowed more aggressive strikes on Iran, sending oil prices higher again and deepening strain on consumers. In a speech …

items.headline: US Burned 14 Years of Missiles in 30 Days
items.summary: The US fired 850 Tomahawk missiles in 30 days but buys only 57 a year. As munitions run dry, the Pentagon pivots to ground troops in Iran, guaranteeing the Strait of Hormuz stays closed, European gas storage hits crisis levels, and Western energy prices keep …

items.summary:

items.headline: Deterrent or danger: After Iran, are Saudi Arabia and Turkey next in line for nuclear weapons?
items.summary: The nuclear threat in the Middle East is growing due to the Iran war, and not just because nuclear sites in Iran and Israel are being attacked. Other countries may decide they're safer with a nuclear weapon too.

items.headline: HMA Moscow Nigel Casey's interview on UK-Russia relations
items.summary: British Ambassador to Moscow Nigel Casey gave an interview about the state and prospects of relations with Russia to RBC outlet on Friday 27 March 2026.

items.headline: One Crew Member Rescued After American Jet Shot Down In Iran, US Official Tells RFE/RL
items.summary: A US crew member has been rescued by American forces after a jet fighter was shot down over Iran, a US official told RFE/RL, and the search is ongoing for a second, in the first known loss of an American plane to hostile fire since the war began.

items.headline: Rescue Mission Races To Find Crew Member Of US Jet Downed Over Iran
items.summary: Rescue teams continue to search early on April 4 for a US crew member missing after their fighter jet was brought down over Iran, one of two American forces planes reportedly knocked out of the sky in the region a day earlier.

items.headline: ‘US pilot rescued and another still missing’ after fighter jet shot down over Iran; EU prepares for fuel rationing
items.summary: Follow our live blog on the war in the Middle East here:

items.headline: Satellite images begin to show damage wrought by Iran war
items.summary: Satellite images are emerging, offering a view of the Iran war's damage. Ships are seen burning in an Iranian port. Military bases in the region show destroyed buildings. Information on the war's toll has been limited. High-resolution images from various firm…

items.headline: What to know about U.S. shifting targets, new deployments and diplomatic double game as conflict enters Day 30?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran that began with targeted "decapitation" strikes on Feb. 28 has spiralled into a multi-front regional war with no end in sight.

items.headline: IAEA Declares Iran's Nuclear Program Outside International Monitoring for Eight Months
items.summary: IAEA Director-General tells UN Security Council that Iran has prevented access to its declared uranium stockpiles for over eight months since strikes on nuclear facilities, raising unprecedented proliferation concerns. Agency reports only one state without nuclear weapons has accumulated 60% enriched uranium.

dimension_signal.rationale: Complete suspension of nuclear monitoring represents existential gamble with regime survival

items.headline: Pakistan-China Launch Joint Five-Point Peace Initiative for Iran-US War
items.summary: Pakistan Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar and China's Wang Yi announce coordinated diplomatic framework calling for immediate ceasefire, Strait of Hormuz reopening, and protection of civilian infrastructure. Initiative positions Beijing as potential guarantor for Iran peace deal.

dimension_signal.rationale: First major Middle East mediation initiative demonstrates shift toward multipolar diplomacy

items.headline: UN Security Council Splits as Russia-China Challenge Iran Sanctions Legality
items.summary: Security Council votes 11-2 to proceed with Iran sanctions committee meeting despite Russian and Chinese objections. Moscow and Beijing argue snapback mechanism lacks legal basis, while US accuses them of protecting Iran. 1737 Committee remains operational despite procedural challenges.

dimension_signal.rationale: Systematic opposition to Iran accountability mechanisms shows contempt for international law

items.headline: Netanyahu Accepts Trump Gaza Peace Board Despite ICC War Crimes Warrant
items.summary: Israeli Prime Minister joins US-led Gaza reconstruction board alongside Putin and Lukashenko, all under ICC arrest warrants. Move raises concerns about board objectivity as Netanyahu faces war crimes charges for Gaza operations while Palestinians view him as obstacle to peace.

dimension_signal.rationale: Accepting board position while under ICC warrant demonstrates complete rejection of international judicial authority

items.headline: US Congress Rejects War Powers Resolutions on Iran by Narrow Margins
items.summary: Senate defeats 47-53 and House rejects 219-212 war powers resolutions requiring congressional approval for further Iran military action. Trump openly admits avoiding word 'war' to circumvent constitutional requirements, as Congress cedes authority to executive branch.

dimension_signal.rationale: Explicit admission of constitutional avoidance demonstrates contempt for legislative oversight

items.headline: Pakistan Claims Diplomatic Victory Over India as Iran Mediation Elevates Islamabad
items.summary: Pakistan's central role in Iran-US mediation and China partnership demonstrates diplomatic relevance despite economic challenges. Modi relegated to single Trump phone call while Pakistani military chief Munir gains direct White House access, marking shift in South Asian power dynamics.

dimension_signal.rationale: Successful Iran mediation role strengthens military's domestic position and international relevance

items.headline: ICC Issues Fresh Guidelines on Putin Arrest Warrant as Hungary Withdrawal Delayed
items.summary: International Criminal Court confirms Hungary remains bound by Putin arrest warrant until June 2026 despite announced withdrawal. Court spokesperson emphasizes states cannot unilaterally determine soundness of ICC legal decisions, as European officials warn of legal consequences for hosting Putin.

dimension_signal.rationale: Continued diplomatic isolation due to ICC warrant limits international engagement options

items.headline: India-Pakistan Nuclear Escalation Dynamics Shift After May 2025 Crisis
items.summary: Carnegie Endowment analysis reveals Pakistan's National Command Authority meeting during Pahalgam crisis represented deliberate nuclear signaling. Study shows evolution of Islamabad's full-spectrum deterrence doctrine lowering nuclear thresholds, with US intervention preventing escalation.

dimension_signal.rationale: Deliberate nuclear signaling during crisis shows willingness to escalate rapidly to nuclear domain

items.headline: Iran Threatens Complete IAEA Cooperation Withdrawal if UN Sanctions Reimposed
items.summary: Tehran warns it will terminate recent IAEA inspection agreement if Security Council snapback sanctions take effect. Iran has prevented inspectors from accessing nuclear facilities since US-Israeli strikes, with over 400kg of 60% enriched uranium remaining unaccounted for.

dimension_signal.rationale: Threat to end all nuclear monitoring represents escalation of isolation strategy for regime survival

items.headline: Arms Control Association Analysis: US Burned 14 Years of Missile Inventory in Iran War
items.summary: Independent analysis shows US military expended unprecedented quantities of precision-guided munitions in first month of Iran operations. Study raises concerns about sustained military capability and industrial base capacity to maintain extended conflict operations.

dimension_signal.rationale: Rapid depletion of missile stocks suggests insufficient long-term planning for sustained operations
